name: stable nuclear fusion

VINIT.chosen_element: vacuum chamber with cold deuterium-tritium fuel
VINIT.justification: represents the uninitialized void state before any magnetic flux or thermal energy is introduced

TANCH.justification: acts as the closed boundary that contains the plasma and defines the system's operational limits

AFWD.justification: directed transformation toward the ignition threshold, increasing plasma temperature and density

AREV.chosen_element: coolant injection and magnetic field relaxation
AREV.justification: reverse transformation that descends from high-energy states to mitigate thermal runaway or disruption

CLINK.justification: chains the morphisms of magnetic shaping, RF heating, and pellet injection into a continuous operational pipeline

IMSCRIB.justification: self-referential feedback loop where diagnostic arrays recognize and maintain their own stable state without external correction

FSPLIT.justification: branches the continuous state into two distinct control paths: magnetic topology adjustment and thermal regulation

FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes the exact original state vector by integrating magnetic and thermal control signals, satisfying FFUSE(FSPLIT(x)) = x

EVALT.justification: affirmative evaluation that the T-arm control path maintains confinement integrity and net positive energy trajectory

EVALF.justification: negative evaluation that the F-arm detects pressure gradient exceeding stability limits, requiring intervention

ENGAGR.justification: paradice state where stable confinement and unstable pressure gradients coexist simultaneously without immediate resolution

IFIX.chosen_element: neutron flux and material activation log
IFIX.justification: permanent, append-only record of radiation exposure and structural changes that cannot be undone or altered

phase_2.split_input: continuous real-time state vector of plasma density, temperature, and magnetic flux
phase_2.fuse_result: continuous real-time state vector of plasma density, temperature, and magnetic flux

phase_3.void_description: vacuum chamber with un-ionized cold fuel, zero magnetic flux, zero thermal energy
phase_3.true_description: steady-state alpha-heated plasma with net positive energy output and stable magnetic confinement
phase_3.false_description: plasma disruption, rapid thermal quench, loss of magnetic topology, and termination of fusion reactions
phase_3.both_description: edge-localized mode (ELM) cycle, holding both stable confinement and unstable pressure gradient simultaneously during transitional oscillation

steps.domain_action: Initialize vacuum chamber with cold deuterium-tritium fuel — establishes the void state before ignition
Apply initial magnetic confinement and radio-frequency heating — directed transformation toward ignition threshold
Chain heating stages toward ignition threshold — sequential morphism of pellet injection, compression, and RF heating
Branch plasma equilibrium state vector — splits continuous state into magnetic topology and thermal regulation paths
Affirm stable magnetic topology on T-arm — positive evaluation of confinement integrity
Forward morphism of increased plasma density on T-arm — drives system toward sustained burn
Reconstitute exact plasma equilibrium state vector — fuses control and regulation paths back to original input
Chain reconstituted state into sustained burn phase — sequential morphism of alpha-heating and energy extraction
Branch operational state into neutron flux monitoring and thermal load distribution — splits state for safety and efficiency control
Flag thermal overload risk on F-arm — negative evaluation of pressure gradient exceeding stability limits
Reverse morphism of coolant injection to mitigate F-arm risk — descent transformation to restore equilibrium
Reconstitute exact operational state vector — fuses monitoring and mitigation paths back to original input
Hold marginal stability state during ELM cycle — paradice where stable and unstable parameters coexist without resolution
Forward morphism of energy extraction to power conversion — directed transformation of thermal/neutron energy to electricity
Self-referential feedback loop where plasma sensors recognize and maintain their own equilibrium — identity operation closing the control loop
Permanently record neutron flux data and material activation levels — irreversible fixation of operational witness
Seal the magnetic boundary and terminate the operational cycle — terminal anchor containing the whole system

phase_5.compiler_frontend: plasma control system (PCS) translating first-principles physics models into actuator commands
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: real-time telemetry bus routing magnetic coil currents, heating power, and diagnostic state vectors
phase_5.memory_mechanism: neutron activation monitors and magnetic flux sensors storing continuous state vectors and historical logs
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: feedback controller prioritizing stability constraints and disruption mitigation over raw power output
phase_5.alfs_store: ITER/DEMO operational database and validated plasma simulation library serving as the canonical bootstrap store

phase_6.cycle_cost: electrical power for superconducting magnets, radio-frequency heating, and coolant circulation
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: cold fuel in vacuum, zero net energy, zero magnetic flux
phase_6.post_cycle_state: helium ash, neutron flux, thermal energy in coolant loops, and activated structural materials
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The control loop operates as a closed thermodynamic cycle where input work exactly balances confinement maintenance and energy extraction, maintaining steady-state equilibrium without net entropy accumulation.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → FFUSE → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → ENGAGR → AFWD → IMSCRIB → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: stable nuclear fusion
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,4,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=3
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=17
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 6), (8, 11)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [6] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [7] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [8] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [9] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [10] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [11] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [12] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑾  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [13] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑳 → 𐑠  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [14] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑾 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription
--   [15] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑠 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [16] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def stable_nuclear_fusion_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize vacuum chamber with cold deuterium-tritium fuel — establishes the ...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Apply initial magnetic confinement and radio-frequency heating — directed tra...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [2] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain heating stages toward ignition threshold — sequential morphism of pelle...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch plasma equilibrium state vector — splits continuous state into magnetic topology and thermal regulation paths) / FFUSE [6] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Affirm stable magnetic topology on T-arm — positive evaluation of confinement...)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism of increased plasma density on T-arm — drives system toward ...)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [6]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [6] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reconstitute exact plasma equilibrium state vector — fuses control and regulation paths back to original input)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑚)  -- [7] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain reconstituted state into sustained burn phase — sequential morphism of ...)
  -- FSPLIT [8] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch operational state into neutron flux monitoring and thermal load distribution — splits state for safety and efficiency control) / FFUSE [11] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙)  -- T-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.T)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Flag thermal overload risk on F-arm — negative evaluation of pressure gradien...)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Reverse morphism of coolant injection to mitigate F-arm risk — descent transf...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [11]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [11] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reconstitute exact operational state vector — fuses monitoring and mitigation paths back to original input)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑾)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Hold marginal stability state during ELM cycle — paradice where stable and un...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑳 𐑠)  -- [13] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism of energy extraction to power conversion — directed transfor...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑾 𐑭)  -- [14] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Self-referential feedback loop where plasma sensors recognize and maintain th...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [15] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Permanently record neutron flux data and material activation levels — irrever...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [16] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the magnetic boundary and terminate the operational cycle — terminal anc...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def stable_nuclear_fusion_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (stable_nuclear_fusion_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def stable_nuclear_fusion_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (stable_nuclear_fusion_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem stable_nuclear_fusion_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

end Imscribing
